articles.title: Stock market today: Sensex falls 114 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,300; mid, small-cap indices jump 1%

articles.title: Bagmane Prime Office REIT IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed 3.48x so far. Check GMP, issue details.
articles.summary: Bagmane Prime Office REIT, backed by Blackstone, opened its IPO for subscription on May 5, concluding on May 7. The price band is set at ₹95-100 per unit, with a strong subscription status of 3.48x as of day 3.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CarTrade Tech shares climb 17% to 2-month high after strong Q4 results
articles.summary: CarTrade Tech has seen a 17% stock surge, reaching a two-month high following strong Q4 and FY26 performance. The company reported a net profit of ₹70.85 crore, a 54% YoY increase, and achieved its highest annual revenue of ₹870 crore, reflecting robust growth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO listing in focus. Here's what GMP signals ahead of debut
articles.summary: OnEMI Technology Solutions IPO, which raised ₹900 crore, will debut on 8 May after strong demand with 9.5 times overall subscription. The issue included a fresh equity offering of ₹850 crore and an offer-for-sale of ₹76 crore. Grey market premium indicates a strong listing price expectation.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bajaj’s export strength may not be enough to justify steep valuation premium over Hero
articles.summary: Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp reported impressive Q4FY26 earnings, with Ebitda growth of 36% and 31%, respectively. Both companies successfully navigated high input costs through strategic pricing and robust sales. What factors contributed to their strong performance and what challenges lie ahead?&nb
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: KKR-backed InCred Holdings files updated IPO papers; valuation may touch ₹15,000 crore
articles.summary: InCred Holdings's offering comprises a fresh issue of equity shares totalling ₹1,250 crore and an offer for sale of up to 99 million shares from existing investors, per draft IPO papers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Can gold prices outperform silver amid weak global macroeconomic outlook?
articles.summary: Gold is likely to outperform silver in the near term as weakening global economic conditions and geopolitical uncertainties continue to drive investors toward safe-haven assets, analysts said.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Vodafone Idea, YES Bank, GTL Infrastructure, HFCL, Meesho among the most traded stocks today, 7 May 2026
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea, YES Bank, GTL Infrastructure, HFCL, Meesho, Jaiprakash Power Ventures (JP Power), Filatex Fashions, Tata Silver Exchange Traded Fund, and Suzlon Energy were among the most traded stocks on the NSE.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Nagaraj Shetti recommends Anant Raj, Rico Auto shares to buy in the short-term
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex showed minor gains on May 7, aided by declining crude oil prices. The Nifty 50 was up 0.07% at 24,355. Analysts predict potential upside targets of 24,600-24,800 in the coming week.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Recode Studios IPO GMP signals 27% listing pop — Issue booked 104x so far on Day 3. Check key details
articles.summary: Recode Studios IPO GMP was ₹43 per share, suggesting that its shares were trading at ₹201 in the unofficial market. At the prevailing GMP, Recode Studios shares could list at a premium of 27.22% as against the offer price of ₹158.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Polycab’s outlook brightens after FY26 market share gains
articles.summary: Polycab’s shares have gained 34% from their early April lows. Margin recovery with sustained revenue growth would be critical for the stock.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bharat Forge Q4 results 2026: Profit falls 17.5% YoY to ₹232.57 crore; recommends final dividend of ₹6.50 for FY26
articles.summary: For the full year FY26, the company's profit rose by 14.72% to ₹1,079.66 crore from ₹941.15 crore in FY25, while revenue from operations climbed by 11.17% to ₹16,811.65 crore from ₹15,122.80 crore in FY25.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Promoter Shareholding: Godrej Properties, Adani Energy see stake hike; BHEL, Vishal Mega Mart, Biocon face cuts
articles.summary: Promoter Shareholding: The companies that recorded an increase in promoter ownership between the December 2025 and March 2026, Godrej Properties saw promoter holding rise from 47.2% to 51.7%, while Adani Energy Solutions witnessed an increase from 71.2% to 72.7%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Upcoming IPO: InCred Holdings files updated draft papers with SEBI for IPO
articles.summary: Upcoming IPO: InCred Holdings Ltd has filed its Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus-I with SEBI, moving closer to its IPO. The offering includes ₹1,250 crores in new equity shares and an offer for sale of 99,020,833 shares, primarily to invest in its subsidiary, InCred Finance.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Navratna PSU BEL announces board meet date for Q4 results and dividend. Details here
articles.summary: Navratna PSU BEL will announce its Q4 results and final dividend later this month. The stock opened at ₹439.90 per share today, as compared to the previous close of ₹438.20 on Wednesday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Indian stock market: Nifty 50 bounces back from support; can a US-Iran peace deal push the index to 25,000 this month?
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 looks set for extending gains for the second consecutive week as geopolitical risks ease. However, elevated crude oil prices remain a key risk. Experts believe oil prices may take months to come down to levels seens at the beginning of the year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Polycab India share price jumps over 6% after strong Q4 results
articles.summary: Polycab India shares rose over 6% after reporting strong Q1 results, with consolidated revenue at ₹8,865 crore, a 27% YoY increase. The wires and cables sector grew 30%, while fast-moving electrical goods saw a 47% rise, despite a 15% decline in the EPC sector.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Smallcaps, midcaps are running too hot after April rally
articles.summary: Analysts warn both segments are richly valued after April’s surge, though mid-caps appear better placed than small-caps to withstand further volatility.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Do you know? 5 common mistakes that can drain your portfolio and how to avoid them
articles.summary: Effective trading requires a clear strategy that avoids emotional pitfalls. Key mistakes include failing to understand market cycles and overtrading. Investors should focus on risk management, establish entry and exit criteria, and avoid impulsive decisions to minimise losses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: IIFL Capital share price zooms over 4% after issuance of preferential shares to Fairfax
articles.summary: IIFL Capital share price opened at ₹335 apiece today, as compared to the previous close of ₹332.50 on Wednesday and rose to the day's high of ₹359 after Fairfax said it would raise its stake in the company. Here are all the details about the issue, along with the share price trend.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Value 360 Communications IPO allotment date likely today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status of SME IPO
articles.summary: Value 360 Communications IPO date is likely today, 7 May 2026. Value 360 Communications allotment status online check can be done through the websites of NSE and the IPO registrar. Kfin Technologies is the Value 360 Communications IPO registrar.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver prices today: Check retail rates of 24K, 22K gold, 999 silver on 7 May in Delhi, Mumbai and Kolkata
articles.summary: MCX gold and silver prices rose on 7 May amid hopes of a US-Iran peace deal and easing crude oil prices. Check the latest 24K, 22K gold and 999 silver rates in Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai and other major Indian cities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying Bharat Dynamics, Max Financial shares to buy - 7 May
articles.summary: The Indian stock market opened positively on May 7, with the BSE Sensex up 380.72 points and the Nifty 50 rising 67.55 points. Market breadth was positive, led by gains in various sectors, while volatility in crude oil prices moderated overall gains.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Paytm share price jumps 6% after fourth straight quarterly profit: More upside ahead?
articles.summary: Shares of fintech player One97 Communications, parent of Paytm, rose 6% in early deals on Thursday, 7 May, after the company posted a profit for the fourth quarter in a row.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Meesho share price jumps nearly 8% after Q4 results. Should you buy or sell?
articles.summary: Meesho share price rose nearly 8% after announcing a narrowed net loss of ₹166.34 crore for Q4 FY26, down from ₹1,391.38 crore a year prior. Revenue surged 47.13% to ₹3,531.21 crore, with annual transacting users increasing by 33% to 264 million.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bajaj Auto share price rallies over 3% to 52-week high after Q4 results. Should you buy, sell or hold the stock?
articles.summary: Bajaj Auto share price has gained 17% in one month, and 11% in three months. The stock has rallied 21% in six months and has surged 35% in one year. Bajaj Auto shares have delivered multibagger returns of 174% over the past five years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank gets RBI nod to acquire ‘aggregate holding’ of up to 9.99% in AU Small Finance Bank
articles.summary: AU Small Finance Bank (SFB) informed exchanges that leading private lender Kotak Mahindra Bank has received Reserve Bank of India's nod to acquire up to 9.99% stake in the company.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Crude oil prices steady as investors weigh US-Iran peace deal; Brent at $102/bbl. Where are prices headed?
articles.summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures rose 88 cents, or 0.9%, to $102.15 per barrel by 0032 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude advanced $1.12, or 1.2%, to $96.20 a barrel.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold rate up amid focus on potential US-Iran peace deal; dimming US Fed rate cut hopes cap gains
articles.summary: Gold rate rose on MCX on Thursday morning. The big relief for gold traders is the prospects of a peace deal between the US and Iran, which can lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil trade.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 13 paise lower at 94.74 against the US dollar
articles.summary: The rupee opened lower at 94.74 against the US dollar, following a prior gain. Falling crude oil prices and geopolitical optimism initially supported the rupee, but concerns emerged after comments from Trump about US-Iran talks. India's economic indicators remain positive despite global uncertainty.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.summary: Q4 results 2026: Around 90 companies will declare their March quarter results on Thursday. Checkout Q4 previews, along with full list of companies to release Q4 results today.

articles.title: Powering the future: Four ancillary stocks driving India’s energy infrastructure boom
articles.summary: As electricity demand turns structural, cables, transformers and equipment makers, not generators, are emerging as the market’s most consistent compounders.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sensex, Nifty 50 | Stock Market LIVE: Sensex, Nifty reverse gains, slip marginally — broader markets outperform
articles.summary: Sensex, Nifty 50 | Stock Market LIVE: Among sectors, Nifty Auto, Nifty Media, Nifty Metals, Nifty Pharma and Nifty IT witnessed gains, while Nifty FMCG, Nifty Realty and Nifty Private Banks declined.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil prices steady as US, Iran consider fresh proposal for peace deal
articles.summary: Oil prices steadied after an 8% drop as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal lifted sentiment, though conflicting signals from Donald Trump capped gains.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sebi’s policy u-turns raise questions on consultations, regulatory certainty
articles.summary: Sebi's recent policy changes have sparked concerns over regulatory uncertainty, with four decisions reversed after stakeholder feedback. Critics emphasize the importance of consistent consultation, which was missing in some two instances.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Welfare spending surge pushes subsidy outgo to 91% of target
articles.summary: Food subsidy, the largest component, stood at ₹1.92 trillion, or 85% of the revised estimate of ₹2.28 trillion. However, the urea subsidy has already breached its annual target, reaching ₹1.28 trillion, or 101% of the revised estimates, and higher than the ₹1.16 trillion recorded a year ago.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How India’s growth story has left its workers behind
articles.summary: Noida’s unrest highlights India’s structural crisis: skipping manufacturing for services has left a low-skilled workforce with stagnant real wages, poor living and working conditions, and diminishing bargaining power.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India’s services sector recovers lost ground, domestic market fuels growth in April: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: India's services sector showed recovery in April, with the HSBC PMI rising to 58.8. Increased output and new business orders were noted, alongside a shift to domestic suppliers. The composite PMI also rose to 58.2, indicating growth momentum across sectors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US Envoy Sergio Gor confirms Marco Rubio will visit India soon to ‘build even stronger ties’
articles.summary: US envoy Sergio Gor announced that Indian companies plan to invest over $20.5 billion in various sectors, contributing to job creation and stronger supply chains. The SelectUSA Investment Summit promotes foreign direct investment, connecting global business leaders with US economic developers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: West Asia war may briefly dent India's growth as fundamentals strong: Memani
articles.summary: CII President Rajiv Memani, who is also the regional managing partner of EY Africa-India and chair of EY Growth Markets Council, said that the challenge before businesses is to strike a balance between the costs that they can absorb and those that must be passed on to consumers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt clears ₹50,000-crore package to boost credit flow and support businesses
articles.summary: The government-backed loan support scheme will facilitate an additional credit flow of up to ₹2.55 trillion to the economy, including ₹5,000 crore specifically for the aviation sector, a government statement said.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SCBs clock 15.9% credit growth in FY26, led by services, MSMEs, retail
articles.summary: The 497-basis-point jump reflects sustained economic momentum and rising demand for credit across sectors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India plans to award construction-ready highway contracts, with all approvals in place, to curb delays
articles.summary: In a bid to eliminate persistent highway project delays, MoRTH is proposing stricter pre-award filters, ensuring all critical land and structural clearances are in place before contracts are awarded. This shift aims to make highway projects more execution-ready.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why RBI wants to keep India's gold at home
articles.summary: RBI repatriated over 100 tonnes of gold to India in the six months to March, taking the total gold reserves stored locally to 680 tonnes. Why is the central bank bringing gold back home?
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India among most resilient as global shocks test emerging markets: Moody’s Ratings
articles.summary: India and Thailand are well placed to manage future shocks because monetary policy frameworks are clear and predictable, inflation expectations are well anchored, and exchange rates can adjust when needed, Moody’s Ratings said.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India’s fertilizer subsidy reaches ₹2.17 trillion in FY26 due to West Asia war, may rise by a fifth in FY27
articles.summary: As the war in West Asia disrupts supply chains, India's fertilizer subsidy may exceed ₹2.17 trillion in FY27. This trend raises concerns about fiscal discipline and food inflation.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI reshuffles top deck: New DG Rohit Jain takes charge, portfolios realigned
articles.summary: Rohit Jain has been appointed deputy governor of the Reserve Bank of India, leading to a portfolio reshuffle among governors. The changes, effective 4 May, aim to enhance oversight of fintech and adapt to evolving financial priorities amid global volatility.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre plans 3.25 mn ha shift to chemical-free farming by FY31
articles.summary: The department of expenditure in coordination with the agriculture ministry, is drafting a proposal to create 65,000 clusters of 50 hectares each under the National Mission on Natural Farming
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Indian private sector new order growth quickens in April but remains slowest in 4 years: PMI survey shows
articles.summary: After losing some momentum in March due to West Asia conflict, growth across India's private sector expanded at a quicker rate at the start of new fiscal year in April.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil prices subdued after Trump says US plans to help stranded ships cross Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices held near $108 as Trump announced ‘Project Freedom’ to guide stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz, though blockade risks and fragile peace prospects kept markets on edge.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rural job scheme demand dips by over a third in April—here’s why
articles.summary: The decline in rural work demand in April may be a continuation of similar declines seen over the past few months, though it could also reflect implementation challenges with the VB G-RAM-G.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil slips after Trump says US will help free ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices fell on Monday after President Trump announced efforts to free stranded ships in the Strait of Hormuz, yet remained above $100 due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude dropped to $107.53, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell to $101.10 amid stalled peace negotiations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt says auto fuel shortage rumour led to panic buys, assures stocks adequate
articles.summary: While cooking gas supplies remained strained due to the West Asia war, household demand had been being prioritized and no shortages have been reported, the petroleum ministry said.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: OPEC members increase oil production quota by 188,000 bpd, no word on UAE's withdrawal amid war in West Asia
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia, Russia, and five other OPEC members increased their oil production quota by 188,000 barrels per day, despite the UAE's recent withdrawal. Analysts suggest this move aims to project stability amid ongoing market disruptions from the Mideast conflict.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Will the West Asia war accelerate the age of electricity? Explained in charts
articles.summary: The 1973 oil crisis dramatically changed the way that economies used fossil fuels such as petroleum. The ongoing US-Iran war could also reshape energy usage—for good.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: New Zealand FTA, steel exports, boardroom gender disparity
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s green energy sector may be hit by power regulator’s stricter performance standards
articles.summary: According to developers, the new deviation rules are hard to follow, since unlike coal or hydro, wind and solar are unstable sources of power.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Eating out, international travel costlier after commercial LPG, ATF price hike
articles.summary: International travel, too, might get more expensive, with prices of aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for international flights being raised, according to a statement by state-run refiner Indian Oil Corporation.
articles.label: NEUTRAL
